highlights: Walk across natural sandbar connecting islands at low tide, visible around 4-5 AM
Fresh seafood meals included with day trips and overnight stays
Peaceful island experience with local families, minimal crowds outside peak hours

thingsToKnow.crowds: Popular Instagram spot during day trips; overnight stays are quieter with fewer visitors
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Speedboat access only via Van Gia port; bumpy rides with high-speed boats; rough sand mixed with stones
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Best visited during dry season (December-June); water very cold in December; sandbar visibility depends on tidal timing

visitorTips: Arrive very early (4-5 AM) to see the full sandbar before it submerges at high tide
Stay overnight for peaceful experience; prepare for no air-conditioning and limited power availability
Pack tolerance for basic facilities, windy conditions, and bumpy boat rides from Nha Trang

faq.question: When is the best time to see the sandbar connecting the islands?
faq.answer: Around 4-5 AM at lowest tide. The sandbar is not always visible and gets submerged at high tide.

faq.question: How much does a boat ticket to Diep Son cost?
faq.answer: Round-trip tickets range from 200,000-300,000 VND per adult from Van Gia port, approximately 20 minutes travel time.

faq.question: What should I expect for accommodation and facilities on the island?
faq.answer: Basic facilities with no air-conditioning, gasoline generators, limited power at night, basic toilets, and shared shower facilities at central areas.

sentiment.positive: Visitors appreciate the peaceful atmosphere, natural landscape, fresh seafood, and unique sandbar experience, especially those with flexible expectations
sentiment.negative: Sandbar visibility has decreased; facilities are basic and underdeveloped; some find the limited activities and rough sand disappointing; food quality inconsistent
